beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, and check on houseplants before the warm day sets in.
beats.reasoning: Viktor wakes early on a good-energy Friday. After yesterday's solid day (energy 45/100), he's ready to move. Morning routine at home before heading to work.

beats.activity: Metro commute from Wedgwood to Capitol Hill, enjoying the mild morning breeze.
beats.reasoning: Transit is back to normal post-strike. Viktor prefers Metro on warm days; it's reliable and lets him decompress before standup.

beats.activity: Work at the indie studio: standup, code review, and iterating on the particle system.
beats.reasoning: Morning block of focused work. The particle system momentum from yesterday carries forward; Viktor is in the zone.

beats.activity: Lunch at a quiet spot in Capitol Hill to avoid the early Pride fair crowds.
beats.reasoning: Viktor grabs lunch solo—he values close friends over big crowds, so he'll skip the street fair surge and eat somewhere calm with a book.

beats.activity: Afternoon work session: debugging and prep for end-of-week code freeze.
beats.reasoning: Solid afternoon push before the Pride closures kick in at 5pm on Broadway/Pike. Viktor stays focused on studio work rather than venture into the fair.

beats.activity: Leave studio early to avoid Pride street closures; head home via alternate route.
beats.reasoning: Broadway and Pike are closed for Pride from 5pm onward. Viktor navigates the closure by heading home early—better than getting stuck. Warm evening perfect for transit.

beats.activity: Quick dinner at home: grilled chicken, salad, and iced water—light meal for a warm evening.
beats.reasoning: Heat-conscious meal choice. Viktor eats light and stays hydrated on a 77F day. He's home early enough to decompress before the evening.

beats.activity: Head to a nearby outdoor court for pickup basketball in the golden-hour light.
beats.reasoning: Warm, breezy evening—perfect conditions for hoops. Viktor loves the golden-hour light and mild breeze. Close enough to Wedgwood for a quick jaunt.

beats.activity: Commute home after basketball, showering off sweat.
beats.reasoning: Quick return to Wedgwood. Viktor values being home by early evening to wind down.

beats.activity: Shower, then settle in with a book on the couch with a cool drink.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening. Viktor reads and relaxes at home—his preferred way to close out a productive day. No big crowds, no late-night commitments.

beats.activity: Sleep, resting after a full day of work and exercise.
beats.reasoning: Solid 9.75-hour sleep. Viktor ends the day in a good place: productive work, exercise, and quiet home time. Ready for Saturday morning.

diary: Woke up early to tend the plants before the heat rolled in—they're looking thirsty this time of year. The commute was pleasant, that breeze hitting just right on the metro. Work flew by; we crushed the particle system review and I caught the code freeze prep before things got too chaotic. Grabbed lunch solo at Café Solstice to dodge the Pride crowds building up outside—didn't feel like fighting the energy downtown just yet. Left the studio early anyway, took the long way home to avoid the street closures on Broadway. Made a light dinner, then headed out to Loyal Heights for pickup basketball. The court was golden in that late afternoon light, and I needed to burn off the week's tension. Shot around for a solid half hour, felt good. Came home, showered, and settled into the couch with a book and something cold. The city hummed with Pride festivities while I sat quiet in Wedgwood. Felt peaceful, honestly.
thought_process: I made a conscious choice to stay peripheral to Pride today—not avoiding it, just respecting the space and the crowds. Work was steady and focused, which kept me grounded through the morning. Leaving early felt smart; I could sense the downtown energy building and knew I'd want solitude after a full day. Basketball was the real pivot point; physical exertion shifted my mood from productive-but-tense to actually settled. The warmth and the breezy conditions made everything feel lighter than usual. I chose the book and quiet evening over joining the fair because I needed to recharge alone, and that felt honest. No FOMO—just a different kind of Friday.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
